steps.title: Count the data set and identify positive numbers
steps.content:
The data set is: $-14$, $-6$, and $31$.
There are 3 total numbers to choose from.
Checking which are positive: $-14$ is negative, $-6$ is negative, and $31$ is positive. So there is 1 positive number.

steps.title: Calculate the probability
steps.content: Probability equals favorable outcomes divided by total outcomes: $$P(\text{positive}) = \frac{1}{3}$$ That matches Choice B — $\frac{1}{3}$.
